ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: "When he asked them, they said, 'Take care of my child!' Dinzuɣu asked them to go and stay until Mahanaim."

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: Naawuni has my inheritance and responsibilities in Omaŋa, and he is the one Naawuni is with, he is the one they know. He has my authority to handle matters for me in Paɣa.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu was born in my village: Nuhu went to ask Ŋun to meet Naawuni, and he told them so that they may know.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: Ka Naawuni said to Ibrahim, "Now is the time that I have finished my work, so I will go to the market now."

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: Amaa, if you take the story from the beginning and fully understand it, and the one who truly sees is Leban, the truthful storyteller, and Yaakubu follows his knowledge, so you understand.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: And he said to them, “We will go without you!” Dinzuɣu then said to the people, “Let us go to Mahanaim.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: Naawuni holds the ninsala and ŋmahindila titles in Omaŋa, and he is the Naawuni they are with, the one they recognize. He has my task to carry out in Paɣa.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.

text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: They said to them, "We are determined to stay here!" Dinzuɣu, they then went to Mahanaim.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: Naawuni has taken the ninsala and ŋmahindila from omaŋa, and he is with Naawuni, his shɛm. He has given it to me to do mini paɣa.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu, my mind is with you: Nuhu has known the wuntizɔra that Naawuni asked, and so he gathered to do it.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: Naawuni said to Ibrahim, "Now is the time, I have not found my companion, truly my path is blocked."

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: Indeed, you have come to my house, my son, to inquire about something, so that you may stay here; and when you came, Lebanon inquired about taking a servant, and Jacob is now with you.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: And he said to them, "Naawuni, protect us!" Dinzuɣu then went to the town called Mahanaim.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: Naawuni provides us with help and sustenance for the community, and he is the one who can assist us. He gives us a way to sustain our lives.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.

translated: Nuhu's thought is in my mind: Nuhu has known the matter that Naawuni inquired about, and has come to tell us today.
